title: RA.1021 Katatonic Silentio
tracklist:
Ariel Kalma - Going Inward / Sound Bath One
Gatha - Spirit Garden
Repetition - Diastolic
CS + Kreme - Devotion
Para - Para Music
Francesco Guccini - Canzone Della Triste Rinuncia

Aris Kindt - Saichh Sequences

translation.content: In 2023, we called Mariachiara Troianiello one of techno's most exciting producers. And time has only confirmed that statement. Belonging to a new school of head-spinning artists following in the lineage of Donato Dozzy and Cio D'or, the Turin producer put out her debut EP as Katatonic Silentio, Emotional Gun, in 2019, exploring breakbeat and IDM through a distinctly introspective lens. Since then, her evolution has been striking: from hyperkinetic, post-rave intensity to the sound design-rich tapestries heard on releases for Delsin, Ilian Tape and Mantis. A spiritual undercurrent has long run through Troianiello's work. Speaking to Minimal Collective, she described her process as an attempt to "spontaneously recreate primordial sounds, such as the sound of water or female voices… the sounds of origins." That sense of returning to something ancient, bodily and intuitive shapes not just her productions, but her approach to DJing too. At first glance, Troianiello's RA Mix ends on an unlikely note: "Technologystolemyvinyle," Moodymann's gloriously disjointed 2007 house cut. But the choice makes perfect sense in context. This is a mix best understood in two halves. The opening stretch leans heavily into acoustic, organic sonics, drifting through Moin-like reverberations and classical detours via Don Cherry and Francesco Guccini. She's spoken before about the guitar as a conduit for that primordial feeling, and here it resonates deeply. Gradually, kick drums emerge as RA.1021 shifts more overtly electronic. Dub pressure seeps in, the atmosphere thickens, and subtle, intricate percussion carries a surprisingly physical weight. Even at its most stripped back, RA.1021 feels full-bodied: immersive, meditative and transportive. There's also an unmistakable sense of freedom throughout, the sound of throwing caution to the wind, playing purely on instinct and joy. It's the feeling of being invited into Troianiello's inner world, and revelling in it together. That unguarded spirit defines RA.1021. - Bella Aquilina What have you been up to recently? I was split between touring and working in the studio to finalise the latest records and live sets. How and where was the mix recorded? And can you tell us the idea behind it? The mix was recorded in my studio with a Xone:92 and two Technics 1200. The idea was to showcase a selection of records from my collection, including some great classics and recent discoveries—things I really like but don't usually play in clubs. It's a mixtape for dreaming but also to dance to. The fil rouge is the sound itself, regardless of genres, tempos and time frames. What are you looking forward to in the near future? 2026 marks my 20th year as a DJ, which is a significant personal achievement in itself. In addition, two particularly meaningful albums will be published in the next months, accompanied by a touring schedule and a couple of exciting collaborations. What's one social or political cause you want the world to pay more attention to? This would deserve a separate chapter. I'd just like to say that politics begins in our own small way; every great gesture and action should spring from small gestures and from who we are in our daily lives. What's one club or party that had a major impact on you as an artist? If I had to choose just one place it'd definitely be Macao in Milan between 2015 and 2020, for its incredible range of electronic and experimental music as well as artistic activities of wide scope.
translation.blurb: Sensational ambient techno, dub pressure and acoustic visions, sculpted for dreaming and dancing.
